document.source_title: World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees
document.summary: A recent attack in Ukraine resulted in the deaths of at least two civilians and significant destruction of humanitarian aid. The UNHCR reported that a warehouse was struck by a ballistic missile, destroying over $1 million worth of aid supplies. Meanwhile, the Greater Horn of Africa faces a forecast of below-average rainfall, raising concerns for food security and public health. Additionally, the UN has launched a $710 million appeal to support Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh amidst rising humanitarian needs.
key_takeaways: Two civilians killed in a missile attack on a UNHCR warehouse in Ukraine.
Over $1 million worth of aid supplies destroyed in the attack.
The Greater Horn of Africa is predicted to experience below-average rainfall from June to September.
The UN is appealing for $710 million to support Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh.
Rohingya refugee needs are rising, with 150,000 new arrivals since early 2024.
Humanitarian agencies are under pressure due to limited resources and rising needs.
document.why_it_matters: The events highlight the ongoing humanitarian crises in Ukraine and the Greater Horn of Africa, as well as the urgent need for support for Rohingya refugees. The destruction of aid supplies in Ukraine underscores the dangers faced by humanitarian workers, while the rainfall forecast in East Africa could severely impact food security and health. The appeal for Rohingya refugees reflects the increasing strain on humanitarian resources and the necessity for international support.

claims.claim_text: At least two civilians were killed in a missile attack in Ukraine.
claims.evidence_text: Bernadette Castel-Hollingsworth said at least two civilians were killed and many more were injured in the 'horrific attack'.

claims.claim_text: Over $1 million worth of aid supplies were destroyed in the attack.
claims.evidence_text: Preliminary estimates revealed that some 900 pallets of aid items... valued at more than $1 million were destroyed in the attack.

claims.claim_text: The UN is seeking $710 million to support Rohingya refugees.
claims.evidence_text: The United Nations and partners are seeking $710.5 million to support more than a million Rohingya refugees living in camps in Bangladesh.

entities.name: UNHCR
entities.description: The UN Refugee Agency involved in humanitarian efforts.
entities.evidence_text: UNHCR and its partners were planning to distribute these supplies to evacuees.

entities.name: Bangladesh
entities.evidence_text: More than 1.2 million Rohingya... have found shelter in neighbouring Bangladesh.

entities.name: Greater Horn of Africa
entities.description: Region facing a forecast of below-average rainfall.
entities.evidence_text: Weather and climate experts predict a high likelihood of below-average rainfall for the northern Greater Horn of Africa.

events.event: Missile attack on UNHCR warehouse in Ukraine
events.evidence_text: Bernadette Castel-Hollingsworth said at least two civilians were killed and many more were injured in the 'horrific attack'.

events.event: Start of critical rainy season in Greater Horn of Africa
events.evidence_text: The outlook was issued by the East Africa-based IGAD Climate Prediction and Applications Centre (ICPAC).

events.event: Launch of $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees
events.evidence_text: The appeal launched in Dhaka on Wednesday is 26 per cent lower than in 2025.
